content.rendered:
How It Started

Linda Knowles is a Florida-based painter and gallery owner known for her vibrant, expressive work. While her exhibitions brought in strong local interest, her online presence didn’t reflect the quality of her brand—or make it easy for new audiences to discover her.

She wasn’t just looking for a basic website; she needed a digital foundation built for growth.

After a discovery call and reviewing a few examples of our creative service projects, Linda chose our top-tier package to bring her gallery online with style and substance.

Her goals were simple and strategic:

  • Make her artwork accessible to a wider audience
  • Sell select prints and merchandise
  • Promote upcoming shows and exhibitions
  • Build something she could manage on her own

Project Overview

Our goal was to:

  • Showcase their portfolio in a visually compelling, mobile-friendly layout
  • Allow for easy updates without needing a developer
  • Create clear areas for promoting new exhibits, works, or educational content
  • Build SEO-ready foundations for future growth

What We Did

Phase 1: Web Design & Structure

  • Designed a clean, visually driven site layout using WordPress + Elementor
  • Organized sections to highlight artwork, upcoming shows, and personal story
  • Used calming color palettes and spacing that matched their artistic identity

Phase 2: Functionality

  • Added upload-ready product pages (prints, originals, etc.)
  • Embedded Google Maps and a contact form for gallery visits and inquiries
  • Installed newsletter signup and optional event calendar tool

Phase 3: SEO Prep

  • Installed Rank Math SEO plugin and connected sitemap
  • Added alt text, page titles, and meta descriptions
  • Connected site to Google Search Console for indexing

Results

While it’s still early to measure long-term results, Linda Knowles now has a polished online gallery that matches the quality of her work. The new website gives collectors and visitors a clear, elegant way to explore her art and get in touch. We also coordinated an on-site photo session with Thomas to capture her artwork and studio space, helping bring the site to life visually.

Next Steps

We’re available for support with:

  • Adding blogs or portfolio updates
  • Connecting email marketing tools
  • Uploading new work for sale

Want your own artist site that blends design and functionality? Book a call with us to get started.
